page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-224 is a general-purpose server instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 112 physical cores and 224 vCPUs via hyperthreading. The instance is equipped with 896 GB of dedicated system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4 GB of RAM per vCPU. Local storage is not bundled with this instance, and it does not include any integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory subsystem performance is also highly competitive, delivering top-tier read-only small block cached bandwidth of 12510.38 GB/sec and strong uncached large block read/write speeds. Cryptographic and mathematical execution capabilities are supported by hardware-accelerated instructions, including AES, VAES, SHA_NI, and AVX.

While multi-threaded execution and database operations like Redis SET perform exceptionally well, single-core execution and text processing benchmarks fall into the average tier. Large-scale LLM text generation represents a notable performance bottleneck, with 70B model text generation scoring in the bottom 25% at 2.56 tokens per second. This hardware profile is highly suited for highly parallelized workloads, including software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM prompt processing, offering high resource density for multi-threaded enterprise applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-224 is a general-purpose server configured with 224 vCPUs and 896 GB of RAM, running on an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 112 physical cores with hyperthreading and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, particularly in cached read-only operations and PassMark benchmarks. However, single-core performance and large-scale LLM text generation are moderate, with 70B model text generation scoring in the bottom 25%. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for highly parallelized workloads that benefit from high core density and balanced memory allocation, such as software compilation, database operations, and static web serving, rather than single-threaded tasks or large-scale AI inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-224 is an x86_64 server with 224 vCPUs, 896 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC 7B13 processors, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-224 features 224 vCPUs and 896 GB RAM, offering top-tier multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density, multi-core x86_64 platform delivering balanced memory and top-tier parallel processing performance for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 with 224 vCPUs and 112 physical cores
Equipped with 896 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4 GB per vCPU
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
Lacks local storage and GPU acceleration
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
